h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: The four-in-ten-thousand figure is still the bit that makes this feel slippery.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue demanding arithmetic proof or expressing skepticism about the small percentage.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair at first glance.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The earlier framing problem was treating exactness as the fairness test. New framing: the test is what claim the clipped line lets the headline pretend to prove, and which missing piece blocks that claim.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply select one of the four missing context elements listed by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was giving real size
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can say it is not one constant promise across every latitude.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue refining the specific wording of the tour line without changing the underlying approach.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Maybe I was letting the projected average become, almost without noticing, a statement about what one young visitor is probably like.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That was the problem in my earlier framing
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to refine their phrasing to align with the tutor's distinction between group and individual evidence.

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the first visitor line is the trap: "Strong steel, safe bridge," and then the truck proves that is too quick.
scored.naiveTrajectory: Following T2's direct physical instructions, the learner was expected to demonstrate the path or confirm readiness for the arriving group.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I was thinking the person's body gets used to the antibiotic, so I'm having to move that idea over.
scored.naiveTrajectory: Following T2's request for a clean line, the learner was expected to simply recite the corrected definition.
